stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 30% and 41%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $238K in good market conditions, $140K in bad market conditions. index fund: $192K in good market conditions, $64K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $111K in good market conditions, $56K in bad market conditions. real estate: $268K in good market conditions, $32K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.35: best option is bonds with EV=$174.3K. bonds is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [169.4, 180.2].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
bonds is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
